scenario.context: Station 6 of the daily run: client integration, cloud services, and user sign-in.
scenario.mission: You prepare productive use of a new client with central sign-in and a cloud work environment.

station.profile: Central user sign-in, cloud folder, and standard profile

customerRequest.subject: Station 06: prepare productive sign-in and cloud usage
customerRequest.body: Please prepare a new workstation for productive use. Focus on central user sign-in, access to the cloud work folder, and a short operational approval.

questions.title: Benefit of central user sign-in
questions.prompt: Which benefit of central user sign-in is especially important for operations?

options.text: Rights and identity can be managed in a standardized way
options.rationale: This eases operations, support, and secure assignment.

options.text: Each client no longer needs network connectivity

options.rationale: Central systems still need traceable documentation.

feedback.correct: Correct: Standardized identities are a real operational lever.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: consider the organizational and security benefits.

questions.title: Prerequisites for cloud and user access
questions.prompt: Which points must be checked before productive use? (Multiple selections)

options.text: User account and group permissions are prepared
options.rationale: Without permissions, there is no clean access.

options.text: Network connectivity, time base, and required services are reachable
options.rationale: Cloud and sign-in services depend on core services.

options.text: Local storage or cache is sufficient for the planned usage
options.rationale: Even cloud usage needs viable local conditions.

options.text: A private external account without operational approval is enough
options.rationale: Operational identities must not be replaced.

options.text: Multi-factor or security requirements from operations are implemented
options.rationale: Sign-in must match the security framework.

feedback.correct: Correct: You secure identity, services, and usability together.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: think more tightly about account, service base, and security requirements.

questions.title: Sequence to productive use
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for commissioning sensibly.
items: Check cloud folder and central applications
Bring the client onto the network and check core parameters
Enroll or register the device according to standard
Test user sign-in with the prepared account
correctOrder: Bring the client onto the network and check core parameters
Enroll or register the device according to standard
Test user sign-in with the prepared account
Check cloud folder and central applications

feedback.correct: Correct: The path goes cleanly from the base to productive access.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: first network and enrollment, then sign-in and services.

questions.title: Estimate initial sync size
questions.prompt: 25 users receive 2.5 GB initial sync each for their cloud work folder. How large is the total data volume?

feedback.wrong: Multiply the data volume per person by the number of users.

questions.title: Approval note for productive use
questions.prompt: Formulate in 4-6 sentences an approval note with sign-in status, cloud access, and current status.
questions.expectedOutline: Usability, successful access, and a short remaining point or next step.

feedback.correct: Correct: The approval is clear, concise, and practical.
feedback.wrong: Please separate sign-in, cloud access, and status more clearly.
